sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.8 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Let$G$denote the group of germs at$+$of orientation-preserving homeomorphisms of the real line${ R}$. Let$G$be the germ of$x x+1.$What are the germs$G$for which the subgroup$,$of$G$generated by$$and$$is free of rank~$2$? If$$is the germ of$x x^k$for an odd integer$k 3$, it is known that$,$is free of rank 2. The proofs of this rely on Galois theory (for$k$an odd prime: S.\,White, J.~Algebra, 118 (1...
